design_philosophy.core_loop: Construct base, harvest resources, build military units, assault opponent base to achieve victory
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Real-time resource management versus opponent expansion; decision between economic growth and military readiness
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Command a faction in near-future global conflict; direct large-scale military operations and territorial conquest
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Introduced full-motion video cutscenes with ensemble cast for narrative delivery (opposed to in-engine rendering); parallel faction campaigns with diverging storylines; distinctive sidebar UI paradigm; faction-specific unit rosters and playstyles rather than symmetrical gameplay

difficulty.checkpointing: Mission-based progression; individual campaign missions serve as progression gates
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Skirmish mode against AI opponents with implied difficulty scaling; multiplayer matches allow players to choose opponents and match parameters

progression.upgrade_system: Tech trees unlocked through structure construction; faction-specific technology advancement gates unit availability
progression.unlock_mechanism: Completing campaign missions progresses narrative and unlocks subsequent missions; constructing new structures in skirmish/multiplayer unlocks new unit types
replayability_hooks: Parallel campaigns for different factions with distinct storylines and unit rosters
Skirmish mode against AI with variable opponent selection
Multiplayer via LAN and modem connection
Faction-specific strategies and unit compositions reward experimentation

world_building_techniques: Full-motion video cutscenes with ensemble cast between missions
Parallel faction campaigns (GDI vs Brotherhood of Nod) presenting conflicting perspectives on central conflict
Mission briefings and objectives contextualizing player actions within larger war narrative
Fictional near-future setting with Tiberium contamination as central world element

faction_indicators: Global Defense Initiative (GDI): UN-formed, containment-focused, military-industrial
Brotherhood of Nod: Cult quasi-state, revolutionary ideology, seeks to harness Tiberium
danger_signals: Tiberium contamination as existential threat driving conflict
Kane as enigmatic cult leader figure representing authoritarian revolutionary movement
Resource scarcity (Tiberium harvesting) creates zero-sum competition
environmental_storytelling: Terrain and map design reflects Tiberium-contaminated world
Faction-specific architecture and unit aesthetics differentiate ideological positions
Mission locations and objectives reveal territorial control and strategic priorities
